scotland_office.name: SCOTLAND OFFICE AND OFFICE OF THE ADVOCATE GENERAL

nhs_provider_overlap.description: NHS Trust funding shown both in DHSC > NHS Trusts (commissioner view) and NHS Provider Sector top-level node (provider/spending view). Same money, two views.
nhs_provider_overlap.residual_explanation: Capital expenditure (PDC, capital loans), HEE training income, and trusts excluded from the TAC sample — items present in DHSC's NHS Trusts allocation but not in TAC EXP0390 operating expenditure.
nhs_provider_overlap.applied_via: scripts/net_nhs_overlap.js (sets _netted=true on DHSC > NHS Trusts node)
nhs_provider_overlap.method: Option B: surgical deduction. DHSC > NHS Trusts reduced by exactly the NHS Provider Sector value, leaving residual. ICB sub-allocations (42 children from enrich_uk_nhs.js) removed because parent value no longer matches and the same money is now represented at top level with sector × trust granularity.

note: Three sources of double-counting: (1) Barnett Formula block grants (£95B) appear in both Central Government and devolved government budgets. (2) NHS Trust funding (£131B) shown in both DHSC commissioning view and NHS Provider Sector spending view — netted via inject_nhs_trusts.js + net_nhs_overlap.js. (3) MHCLG Local Government grants (£34B) overlap with LA England branch (disclosure only, not deducted from tree).
source: HM Treasury OSCAR/OSCAR II + NHS England TAC 2023/24 + MHCLG Revenue Outturn

history.change: Added nhs_provider_overlap section. Root dropped from £1,383B to £1,380B after NHS netting. Total fiscal consolidation deductions now £225B (16.3% of root vs 6.8% pre-netting).
